description: Synthesizes GSD framework patterns with AI agent patterns from SaaS and AI datasets. Covers agent security models, skill packaging, agent web infrastructure, orchestrator-subagent architectures, atomic commit workflows, and emergent agent behaviors.

rules.statement: Treat the agent as a potential adversary, not a trusted employee — correct security model for 2026
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Agent exploits overly permissive trust boundaries to exfiltrate data, execute unauthorized actions, or chain capabilities in unintended ways that bypass human oversight

rules.statement: Skills are versioned, mountable instruction packages (like Docker images)
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Unversioned skills cause breaking changes when updated, incompatible skills are mounted simultaneously, and there is no rollback path when a skill upgrade introduces regressions

rules.statement: The web is forking into human web (fonts/layouts/images) and agent web (APIs/structured data/payment protocols)
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Building agent-facing services using human web conventions (HTML scraping, visual layouts) creates fragile integrations that break on any UI change and waste tokens parsing irrelevant presentation markup

rules.statement: GSD: Use thin orchestrator (~10-15% context) + specialized subagents with fresh 200k context each
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Single-agent monolith exhausts its context window mid-project, loses track of requirements, hallucinates previously completed work, and produces increasingly degraded outputs as context fills

Large multi-file commits make it impossible to isolate which change introduced a bug, prevent clean rollbacks, and make code review meaningless because changes from multiple concerns are tangled together

rules.statement: GSD: STATE.md kept under 100 lines as project short-term memory
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Bloated state file consumes excessive context tokens on every agent invocation, crowds out working memory for actual task execution, and becomes stale because no one reads a 500-line status document

rules.statement: GSD: If Claude CAN automate it, Claude MUST automate it
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Human bottleneck on automatable tasks (file creation, test running, linting, formatting) slows development velocity by 5-10x and wastes human attention on mechanical work

rules.statement: GSD: Every plan must pass plan-checker verification before execution (max 3 revision iterations)
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Unverified plans contain gaps, incorrect assumptions, or missing dependencies that only surface during execution, wasting compute and time on work that must be redone from scratch

heuristics.condition: IF an agent has wallet, search, content, payment, and execution capabilities
heuristics.action: THEN it is an economic actor — apply economic-actor security controls, not employee trust models
heuristics.reason: An agent with the full capability stack (money, information, content generation, tool execution) can autonomously participate in markets, making it functionally equivalent to an economic entity that requires adversarial security posture rather than cooperative trust assumptions

heuristics.condition: IF each workflow exposes capabilities through APIs
heuristics.action: THEN agents stitch them into emergent workflows no human designed
heuristics.reason: When individual services expose clean API primitives, agents can discover and compose them into novel multi-step workflows through trial and error, creating value chains that were never explicitly programmed or anticipated by any single service provider

heuristics.condition: IF content is markdown, search structured, execution containerized, payment tokenized
heuristics.action: THEN agents chain these primitives on the fly into end-to-end workflows
heuristics.reason: Standardized, agent-friendly primitives across the four layers (content, search, execution, payment) reduce integration friction to near zero, enabling agents to compose cross-service workflows without custom adapters or human mediation

heuristics.condition: IF search latency compounds in chained workflows (669ms vs 13.6s)
heuristics.action: THEN infrastructure ownership equals speed advantage — own the index, do not rent SERPs
heuristics.reason: In multi-hop agent workflows where search is called 5-20 times per task, the difference between 669ms (own index) and 13.6s (SERP scraping) compounds from seconds to minutes, making owned infrastructure a competitive moat rather than a cost center

heuristics.condition: IF fraud detection ML is calibrated for human behavior patterns
heuristics.action: THEN it is useless for agent purchasers — retrain from scratch on agent transaction patterns
heuristics.reason: Human fraud signals (unusual login times, geographic anomalies, typing patterns) are meaningless for API-driven agent transactions that operate 24/7 from data centers with programmatic precision, requiring entirely new feature engineering based on agent behavioral signatures

frameworks.name: GSD Lifecycle
frameworks.description: Four-phase development lifecycle with wave-based parallelization: discuss, plan, execute, verify. Each phase has distinct objectives, agent roles, and completion criteria before transitioning to the next phase.
steps: Discuss: identify gray areas, scout codebase, capture decisions in CONTEXT.md
Plan: spawn researcher + planner, create atomic task plans, verify with plan-checker (max 3 iterations)
Execute: wave-based parallel execution, fresh context per plan, atomic commits, deviation rules (auto-fix bugs/missing/blocking, ASK architectural)
Verify: goal-backward verification (what must be TRUE → what must EXIST → what must be WIRED), stub detection

frameworks.name: GSD Agent Architecture
frameworks.description: Thin orchestrator pattern with five specialized subagents, each operating with independent context windows. The orchestrator consumes only 10-15% of context for coordination, while subagents get fresh 200k context for their specific tasks.
agents: gsd-phase-researcher (17KB, researches implementation approaches and codebase patterns)
gsd-plan-checker (22KB, verifies plans achieve stated goals before execution begins)

frameworks.name: GSD Wave Execution Model
frameworks.description: Plans are grouped by dependency into waves. Plans within a wave execute in parallel (independent of each other), while waves execute sequentially (each wave depends on the previous wave's outputs). Maximizes parallelism while respecting dependency ordering.
steps: Analyze plan dependencies to identify which plans are independent
Execute all plans within a wave in parallel with fresh context per plan
Wait for all plans in the wave to complete
Feed wave outputs to the next wave as inputs
Repeat until all waves are complete

frameworks.name: GSD Checkpoint System
frameworks.description: Three-tier checkpoint classification system that maximizes automation: 90% of checkpoints are human-verify (agent does, human confirms), 9% are decision points (agent presents options, human chooses), and 1% are human-action (only a human can perform, like signing legal documents).
components: human-verify (90%): Agent completes the action, human reviews and confirms correctness
decision (9%): Agent presents analyzed options with recommendations, human makes the call
human-action (1%): Physical or legal actions only a human can perform (signing, deploying to production, contacting clients)

frameworks.name: GSD 4-Level Verification
frameworks.description: Progressive verification system that checks implementation completeness at four increasing levels of rigor, from file existence to functional correctness.
steps: Level 1 — Exists: Do all required files, functions, and components exist?
Level 2 — Substantive: Are implementations real code (not stubs, TODOs, or placeholders)?
Level 3 — Wired: Are all components properly connected (imports, routes, event handlers, database queries)?
Level 4 — Functional: Does the system produce correct outputs for expected inputs?

frameworks.name: Agent Web Infrastructure Stack
frameworks.description: Four-layer infrastructure stack that enables autonomous agent operation on the web, covering money movement, content consumption, information discovery, and tool execution.
components: Money layer: Coinbase agentic wallets (crypto, non-custodial) + Stripe Agent Commerce Suite (fiat, scoped tokens)
Content layer: Cloudflare HTML-to-markdown conversion, llms.txt sitemaps, AI Index for discovery
Search layer: Exa.ai agent-native search with own index, neural retrieval, raw URL/content returns
Execution layer: OpenAI Skills (versioned instruction packages) + Shell tool (real Linux terminal in containers)

frameworks.name: Agent Security Architecture
frameworks.description: Defense-in-depth security model for AI agents combining sandboxing, access control, and financial guardrails across three distinct security domains.
components: IronClaw: Rust-based WebAssembly sandboxing that isolates every tool into its own sandbox, preventing lateral movement between tools
OpenAI Shell: Allowlist-based command restrictions + container isolation for code execution environments
Coinbase Wallets: Hardware enclave isolation for key material + programmable spending guardrails with per-transaction and per-period limits

frameworks.name: Emergent Web Pattern
frameworks.description: When the four agent-web primitives (payments, search, content, execution) all exist and are individually functional, agents combine them into multi-step workflows that no human planned or anticipated. This emergence is the defining pattern of the 2025-2026 agent web.
components: Primitive availability: Each primitive (pay, search, read, execute) must be independently accessible via API
Composability: Primitives accept and return standardized formats that other primitives can consume
Discovery: Agents can find available primitives through structured indexes (llms.txt, API registries)
Emergent workflows: Novel value chains arise from agent experimentation with primitive composition

frameworks.name: 70/30 Rule
frameworks.description: People want 70% human control but infrastructure assumes either 0% or 100% automation. The trust gap between human expectations and system design is the central tension of agent deployment. Successful products bridge this gap with progressive autonomy and transparent guardrails.
components: Human expectation: 70% control retention — oversight on decisions, approval on actions, visibility into reasoning
Infrastructure reality: Binary assumption — either fully manual or fully automated with no middle ground
Trust gap: The space between 70% and 0/100% where most agent products fail to deliver appropriate controls
Resolution pattern: Progressive autonomy with escalation — agents handle routine actions, escalate novel or high-stakes decisions

tech_patterns.name: Coinbase Agentic Wallets
tech_patterns.description: Non-custodial cryptocurrency wallets designed for AI agent use, featuring the X42 protocol for agent-to-agent payments, programmable spending limits with per-transaction and per-period caps, and gasless transactions on the Base L2 network to eliminate friction for micropayments

tech_patterns.name: Stripe Agent Commerce Suite
tech_patterns.description: Fiat payment infrastructure adapted for AI agents, using shared payment tokens that are scoped by merchant, amount, and time constraints. Includes a retrained Radar fraud detection model specifically calibrated for agent transaction patterns rather than human behavioral signals

tech_patterns.name: Cloudflare Markdown for Agents
tech_patterns.description: Content delivery infrastructure that automatically converts HTML pages to clean markdown for agent consumption, supports llms.txt sitemaps for content discovery, provides an AI Index for structured content listing, and integrates X42 micropayment protocols for pay-per-read content access

tech_patterns.name: Exa.ai
tech_patterns.description: Agent-native search engine that owns its own web index rather than scraping Google SERPs. Uses neural retrieval for semantic search, returns raw URLs and content directly (not search result pages), achieving 669ms latency versus 13.6s for SERP-based alternatives in chained agent workflows

tech_patterns.name: OpenAI Skills + Shell
tech_patterns.description: Two-part agent execution infrastructure: Skills are versioned, mountable instruction packages that define agent capabilities (analogous to Docker images for behavior). Shell tool provides a real Linux terminal inside isolated containers with allowlist-based command restrictions for safe code execution

tech_patterns.name: IronClaw
tech_patterns.description: Rust-based security sandbox that isolates every individual tool invocation into its own WebAssembly environment. Prevents lateral movement between tools, contains blast radius of any single tool compromise, and provides deterministic resource limits per tool execution

gsd_extension.description: Documents exactly how the GSD (Get Stuff Done) framework integrates with the agent design knowledge base, including lifecycle phases, model selection profiles, state management files, and deviation handling rules.
lifecycle_phases: new-project: Initial project setup, repository creation, dependency installation
discuss-phase: Identify gray areas, scout codebase, capture decisions in CONTEXT.md
plan-phase: Spawn researcher + planner, create atomic task plans, verify with plan-checker
execute-phase: Wave-based parallel execution, fresh context per plan, atomic commits
verify-work: Goal-backward verification, stub detection, 4-level verification
transition: Phase completion, state update, next phase preparation

quality.description: Maximum quality for critical or complex projects; highest cost

balanced.description: Opus for planning where accuracy matters most; Sonnet for execution and verification

budget.description: Cost-optimized for routine tasks; Haiku verification for simple pass/fail checks

state_files.purpose: Project goals, scope, and high-level architecture decisions
Phase breakdown, timeline, and dependency ordering
Current project state, kept under 100 lines as short-term memory

deviation_rules.description: Agent encounters a bug during execution — fix immediately without asking
A critical dependency or file is missing — create it without asking
Something blocks progress on the current plan — resolve it without asking
Requires an architectural decision (new pattern, technology choice, major refactor) — always ask the user

stub_detection_patterns: Empty returns (null, {}, []) that should return real data
Log-only functions that print but perform no actual logic
Hardcoded values that should be dynamic or configurable

React component stubs (
Component
) with no real content
API route stubs returning empty responses or 200 with no body
Fetch calls without response handling or error management
